name: Graph.Colored.Graph

aliases.name: ColoredGraph
aliases.comment:
Describes a graph consisting of Colored Nodes.

See Graph.Colored.Node.

values.name: decode
values.comment: Decode a ColoredGraph from JSON.

values.name: encode
values.comment: Encode a ColoredGraph to JSON.

name: Graph.Colored.Decorator
comment:
This decorator helps you annotate a graph with colors.

@docs coloredGraph

values.name: coloredGraph
values.comment:
Create a Graph with colored nodes from a graph of simple nodes. The colors
will be determined based on the node names (each being a list of strings). The
first parameter (depth) determines how many name components will form a group.

Let assume a graph with the following nodes:

    Node A
    Node B
    Node B.1
    Node B.2

For depth 1 every top level node name will get its own color: B, B.1 and B.2
will have the same color, A will have a different color. For depth 2 all nodes
will have different colors.

This function returns the ColorDict along with the new graph. The color dict is
a look up table for the identifying parts of a name that determine a color.

In our example above for depth 1 that will be: `{ A: "color1", B: "color2" }`.
This can be used to create legends.
values.type: Int -> Graph Node -> ( ColorDict, Graph (Colored Node) )

name: Graph.Json

values.name: decode
values.comment: A decoder for `Graph Node`.

values.name: encode
values.comment: Encode `Graph Node`.

name: Graph.GraphViz
comment:
Exports a Graph in [GraphViz](http://www.graphviz.org) format.

@docs string

values.name: string
values.comment: A GraphViz representation of the Graph.

name: Graph.Degree
comment:
This module provides functions to calculate the degrees of nodes in a graph.

* outdegree: number of outgoing edges
* indegree: number of incoming edges

## Types

@docs Degree, InOut

## Functions

@docs allDegrees, incoming, outgoing, topDegrees

aliases.name: Degree
aliases.comment:
The degree of a node in a graph is the number of edges incident to it.

See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vertex_(graph_theory)

aliases.name: InOut
aliases.comment: Encapsulates information about incoming and outgoing degrees.

values.name: allDegrees
values.comment:
Get a dictionary of all degrees (incoming and outgoing) for all nodes in a
 graph.

values.name: incoming
values.comment: Get the incoming degree of a node in a graph.

values.name: outgoing
values.comment: Get the outgoing degree of a node in a graph.

values.name: topDegrees
values.comment:
Get a list of all top inbound and outbound nodes with their respective
InOut Degrees. The lists are sorting descending by the degree (fallback
alphabetically by identifier).
values.type: Graph Node -> InOut (List ( Identifier, InOut Degree ))

name: Graph.Colored.Node

aliases.name: Colored
aliases.comment: Describes a record that is extended by a color field.

values.name: decode
values.comment: Decode a Colored Node from JSON.

values.name: encode
values.comment: Encode a Colored Node to JSON.

values.name: fromNode
values.comment: Create a colored node from an uncolored node using a color.

name: Graph.Edge.Json

values.name: decode
values.comment: Decodes JSON into an Edge record.

values.name: encode
values.comment: Encodes an Edge record as JSON.

name: Graph

aliases.name: Graph
aliases.comment:
A network graph consists of a list of nodes connected by edges.

The node type of the Graph is abstract to allow you to use records extending the
type Node. Most functions provided by this work with `Graph Node`.
aliases.type: { edges : List Edge, nodes : List node }

values.name: empty
values.comment: Creates an empty graph without edges or nodes.

values.name: filter
values.comment: Filters a graph such that only nodes passing the test are included.

values.name: filterByName
values.comment:
Filter by name to include only nodes that start with the
specified name prefix.

values.name: init
values.comment: Initialize a node from a list of edges (see Edge) and nodes (see Node).
values.type: List Edge -> List Node -> Graph Node

name: Graph.Node.Json

values.name: decode
values.comment: Decodes JSON into a Node record.

values.name: encode
values.comment: Encodes a Node record as JSON.

name: Graph.Colored.Color
comment:
Color can be used to better visualize a graph.

@docs Color, ColorDict, fallback, list

aliases.name: Color
aliases.comment: Color is a type alias for String to improve function signatures.

aliases.name: ColorDict
aliases.comment: ColorDict is a look-up table for colors.

values.name: fallback
values.comment: A fallback color in case you cannot find a color in your ColorDict.

values.name: list
values.comment:
List of decent colors.

see https://material.io/guidelines/style/color.html#color-color-palette

name: Graph.Edge
comment:
An edge connects two nodes in a graph. Edges are designed to work with the
type Node. You may however use an extensible record to add fields to Node
without breaking the Edge implementation.

@docs Edge, filterForIdentifiers

aliases.name: Edge
aliases.comment: Describes the link between two nodes in a Graph.

values.name: filterForIdentifiers
values.comment:
Filter list of edges to only include edges referencing a node in the
given list. This is useful to exclude external nodes that might have been
accidentally referenced.
values.type: Set Identifier -> List Edge -> List Edge

name: Graph.Node

aliases.name: Identifier
aliases.comment: A simple type alias for String to make function signatures easier readable.

aliases.name: Name
aliases.comment:
A node's name is a list of strings to allow
you to represent paths (e.g. for modules).

aliases.name: Node
aliases.comment: Describes a node in a graph.

values.name: dictFromList
values.comment: Get a dictionary to look up nodes for identifiers.

values.name: fromName
values.comment:
Create a node from a name using. An identifier will be created
automatically. Use this function if all nodes in your graph have unique names.

values.name: identifierFromName
values.comment:
Create an identifier from a name. This is only useful if you expect names to
be unique in your graph.

values.name: namePrefix
values.comment: Returns the first *n* members of a name.
